quickFacts: Mozart was born here — you'll see his face on literally everything
The Salzburg Card covers all major sights and public transport
Sound of Music tours are cheesy but genuinely fun
Austrian water quality is incredible — drink from any tap
foodScene: Salzburger Nockerl (sweet soufflé) is the signature dessert. Bosna (spicy sausage in a roll) from the stand at Balkan Grill. Augustiner Bräustübl for the most authentic beer hall experience — you bring food, they pour the beer. Kaffeehäuser for Apfelstrudel.

places.name: Hohensalzburg Fortress
places.caption: Medieval hilltop fortress
places.description: Take the funicular up to one of Europe's largest preserved castles. The views over the city and Alps are stunning. Evening concerts in the state rooms.

places.name: Altstadt (Old Town)
places.caption: Baroque UNESCO centre
places.description: Getreidegasse's wrought-iron shop signs, Mozart's birthplace, and the Residenzplatz fountain. Compact enough to explore in half a day.

places.name: Mirabell Palace & Gardens
places.caption: Do-Re-Mi filming location
places.description: The gardens are free and gorgeous. The Pegasus fountain and hedge garden are Sound of Music filming spots. Marble Hall has free concerts.

places.name: Kapuzinerberg
places.caption: Secret viewpoint hike
places.description: A short steep hike through forest to the best viewpoint in Salzburg. Way fewer tourists than the fortress. Stefan Zweig lived at the top.

places.name: Augustiner Bräustübl
places.caption: Monk-brewed beer hall
places.description: A 600-year-old monastic brewery. Grab a ceramic mug, fill it from the barrel, buy snacks from the food stalls. 1,400 seats and pure atmosphere.

places.name: Salzburg Cathedral
places.caption: Baroque dome & organ
places.description: Mozart was baptised here. The dome is enormous and the organ has 4,000 pipes. The crypt and museum are worth the small fee.

places.name: Museum der Moderne
places.caption: Modern art on the cliff
places.description: Contemporary art museum perched on the Mönchsberg cliff. The art rotates, but the rooftop terrace café views are permanently excellent.

places.name: Untersberg Cable Car
places.caption: Alpine summit in minutes
places.description: Ride to 1,853m for jaw-dropping panoramas of the Alps and the Bavarian plain. Easy hiking trails at the top. Covered by the Salzburg Card.

places.name: Hellbrunn Palace & Trick Fountains
places.caption: Whimsical water gardens
places.description: A 17th-century archbishop built elaborate trick fountains to soak his dinner guests. You will get wet. Genuinely hilarious.

places.name: Day Trip: Hallstatt
places.caption: Fairy-tale lakeside village
places.description: Arguably the most photographed village in Austria. Salt mines, a skywalk, and mirror-calm lake reflections. Go early to beat day-trippers.

places.name: Getreidegasse
places.caption: Medieval shopping street
places.description: Narrow old-town street with wrought-iron guild signs. Mozart's birthplace at No. 9. International brands in medieval buildings.

places.name: Mozart's Birthplace
places.caption: Born here in 1756
places.description: The apartment where Mozart was born and spent his first 17 years. Original instruments, letters and portraits.

places.name: Sound of Music Tour
places.caption: Do-Re-Mi!
places.description: Bus tours to Leopoldskron Palace lake scene, Mondsee wedding church and the Alps. Sing along encouraged.

places.name: Eisriesenwelt Ice Caves
places.caption: World's largest ice caves
places.description: 42 km of ice formations inside a mountain near Werfen. Cable car + hike to enter. Open May-October. Bring warm clothes.

places.name: Stiegl Brewery Tour
places.caption: Austria's largest private brewery
places.description: Interactive brewery experience since 1492. Beer tastings, brewing process and a museum. On the city outskirts.
